title: [FIRRTL][CheckCombCycle] Improve error message
body:
The error message produced by CheckCombCycle is unfriendly compared to SFC (h/t @nibrunieAtSi5). There are two things for improvement in my mind. 

1. Source locations of ports are not useful
The location of a port is the same as its module. Hence if diagnostic is attached to a port, it only shows its module definition. For example, 
```scala
circuit top_mod :
  module top_mod :
    output a: UInt<1>
    output b: UInt<1>
    a <= b
    b <= a
```

MFC Output:
```
foo.fir:2:10: error: detected combinational cycle in a FIRRTL module
  module top_mod :
         ^
foo.fir:2:10: note: this operation is part of the combinational cycle
foo.fir:2:10: note: this operation is part of the combinational cycle
```

We should at least print port names. 

2. Pick a simple loop from SCC as SFC does (https://github.com/chipsalliance/firrtl/commit/e40660622d39995b46c174b56f95f4d6ce3dee02).

Currently, MFC dumps all the operations contained in the SCC. It is sometimes difficult to debug since shown messages do not always represent a simple loop. For example, consider the following IR:
```scala
circuit hasloops :
  module hasloops :
    input i : UInt<1>
    output o : UInt<1>
    wire a : UInt<1>
    wire b : UInt<1>
    wire c : UInt<1>
    wire d : UInt<1>
    wire e : UInt<1>
    a <= and(c,i)
    b <= and(a,d)
    c <= b
    d <= and(c,e)
    e <= b
    o <= e
```

SFC Output:
```
Exception in thread "main" firrtl.transforms.CheckCombLoops$CombLoopException: : [module hasloops] Combinational loop detected:
hasloops.a
hasloops.b
hasloops.c
hasloops.a  
```
MFC Output:
```
mult.fir:2:10: error: detected combinational cycle in a FIRRTL module
  module hasloops :
         ^
mult.fir:10:10: note: this operation is part of the combinational cycle
    a <= and(c,i)
         ^
mult.fir:7:5: note: this operation is part of the combinational cycle
    wire c : UInt<1>
    ^
mult.fir:8:5: note: this operation is part of the combinational cycle
    wire d : UInt<1>
    ^
mult.fir:13:10: note: this operation is part of the combinational cycle
    d <= and(c,e)
         ^
mult.fir:9:5: note: this operation is part of the combinational cycle
    wire e : UInt<1>
    ^
mult.fir:6:5: note: this operation is part of the combinational cycle
    wire b : UInt<1>
    ^
mult.fir:11:10: note: this operation is part of the combinational cycle
    b <= and(a,d)
         ^
mult.fir:5:5: note: this operation is part of the combinational cycle
    wire a : UInt<1>
```
